div.h2: WAL vs Rollback Journal — I/O Pattern Comparison

g.text: Reader during write | BLOCKED by flock(LOCK_EX) | CONCURRENT via snapshot
Writer lock scope | Entire transaction | WAL append lock only
Isolation mechanism | Exclusive file lock | end_frame snapshot integer
Snapshot acquisition cost | Lock syscall | Single atomic int read
Readers block writer | YES — LOCK_SH held | NO — snapshot only
Multiple readers simultaneously | YES (serialized) | YES (fully parallel)
Operation | Journal Random I/O | WAL Sequential I/O
Write target | pwrite to page offset in DB file | pwrite append to WAL end
Seek pattern | N random seeks (1 per dirty page) | 1 seek to WAL EOF
HDD write throughput | ~200 pages/sec (random IOPS) | ~100 MB/s sequential
NVMe comparison | 300 MB/s random vs 3 GB/s seq | 3 GB/s sequential
Write amplification | 2 writes per page (journal + DB) | 1 write per page (WAL)
Post-commit overhead | None (journal unlinked) | Checkpoint copies WAL to DB
Read overhead | None — DB always current | Check WAL index on every read
① read original page into buffer pool
③ fsync(journal_fd) — BARRIER: journal durable before DB touch
⑤ fsync(db_fd) — BARRIER: DB pages durable
⑥ unlink(db-journal) — COMMIT POINT: journal deleted = committed
journal pwrite(s) | random I/O to journal file | 1 call per unique page
fsync(journal_fd) | 5 ms HDD / 0.1 ms NVMe | mandatory before DB write
db pwrite(s) | random seeks across DB file | 1 call per dirty page
fsync(db_fd) | 5 ms HDD / 0.1 ms NVMe | mandatory before journal delete
fsyncs per COMMIT | 2 fsyncs total | DOUBLED latency vs WAL
1000 auto-commit INSERTs | 2000 fsyncs x 5 ms = 10 sec (HDD)
③ walindex_insert(shm, page_no, frame_idx) — update hash map
④ fsync(wal_fd) — SINGLE BARRIER on commit frame only
⑤ COMMIT DONE — main DB file never touched during txn
--- CHECKPOINT (background): copy WAL frames to db_fd ---
WAL pwrite(s) | SEQUENTIAL append — single stream, zero seeks
fsync(wal_fd) | 5 ms HDD / 0.1 ms NVMe | single barrier per commit
db pwrite(s) | ZERO during transaction | deferred to checkpoint
db fsync | ZERO during transaction | deferred to checkpoint
fsyncs per COMMIT | 1 fsync total | HALF the cost of journal
1000 auto-commit INSERTs | 1000 fsyncs x 5 ms = 5 sec (HDD)
Exists ONLY during active write transaction
EXCLUSIVE flock held for entire transaction duration
WAL Hdr | magic=0x377f0682 | salt1 | salt2 | seq_no
Append-only — write pointer never moves backward
page 42 | STALE — last checkpoint version on disk
page 77 | STALE — last checkpoint version on disk
page 13 | STALE — last checkpoint version on disk
Readers access freely — no write lock contention
